description: Print the full, absolute path of the current working directory.
Create a directory, including any necessary parent directories that do not currently exist.

flags.meaning: No error if existing, make parent directories as needed.

description: Recursively and forcefully remove a directory and all of its contents without prompting.

flags.meaning: Remove directories and their contents recursively.
Force deletion; ignore nonexistent files and never prompt.

description: Search the current directory and all subdirectories for a file with a specific name.

flags.meaning: Start the search in the current directory.
Base of file name matches the specified pattern.

description: Read the contents of a file and print it directly to the terminal screen.
Display and update sorted information about processes in real-time.
Display a static snapshot of all currently running processes across all users.

flags.meaning: Show processes for all users.
Display the process's user/owner.
Also show processes not attached to a terminal.

description: Forcefully terminate a process using its numerical Process ID (PID).

flags.meaning: Sends the SIGKILL signal, forcing the process to terminate immediately without cleanup.

description: Forcefully kill all instances of a process by its exact name.

flags.meaning: Sends the SIGKILL signal to immediately terminate the matching processes.

description: Executes a command or script in the background, freeing up the current terminal prompt.

flags.meaning: Placed at the end of a command, it instructs the shell to run the job in the background.

description: Create a new user account and automatically generate their home directory.

flags.meaning: Create the user's home directory if it does not exist.

description: Change or update the authentication token (password) for a specific user.
Switch the current terminal session to another user and load their environment variables.

flags.meaning: Start a login shell, loading the target user's profile and paths.

description: Display the raw file containing all registered user accounts on the system.
Completely remove a user account and delete their home directory and mail spool.

flags.meaning: Remove the user's home directory and files completely.

description: Print comprehensive system information including kernel name, network node hostname, kernel release, and machine hardware name.

flags.meaning: Print all available system information.

description: Report the amount of available disk space being used by file systems.

flags.meaning: Print sizes in human-readable format (e.g., 1K, 234M, 2G).

description: Tell how long the system has been running, how many users are currently logged on, and the system load averages.
Immediately halt the system and trigger a full reboot.

flags.meaning: Requests that the system be rebooted after it has been brought down.
Executes the shutdown sequence immediately without waiting.

description: Start a specific system service or daemon (Note: macOS users utilize 'launchctl' instead of 'systemctl').

flags.meaning: The systemd command to activate the target service.

description: Change the access permissions of a file or directory. 755 grants read/write/execute to the owner, and read/execute to everyone else.

flags.meaning: Numeric representation of permissions: User (7 = rwx), Group (5 = r-x), Others (5 = r-x).

description: Change the user and/or group ownership of a specific file or directory.

flags.meaning: Optional parameter to simultaneously change the group ownership alongside the user.

description: Acquire a root shell environment. It runs the login shell as the target user (root by default).

flags.meaning: Simulates an initial login, reading the root user's profile and setting environmental variables accordingly.

description: Print the effective user ID (username) of the current logged-in user. Useful for verifying privilege levels.
Generate a new SSH key pair for secure authentication to remote servers (e.g., GitHub, AWS). ed25519 is the modern secure standard.

flags.meaning: Specifies the type of key to create. Ed25519 is faster and more secure than legacy RSA.
Provides a comment (usually an email) to easily identify the key later.

description: Send ICMP ECHO_REQUEST packets to network hosts to check connectivity. (Note: UNIX pings infinitely by default, so we limit it to 4).

flags.meaning: Stop after sending exactly 4 ECHO_REQUEST packets.

description: View all active and inactive network interfaces, including local IP addresses and MAC addresses. (On newer Linux, 'ip a' is also used).

flags.meaning: Display all interfaces which are currently available, even if they are down.

description: Print the route and measure transit delays of packets across an IP network.
Print active network connections, routing tables, and listening ports.

flags.meaning: Show TCP connections.
Show UDP connections.
Show only listening sockets.
Show numerical addresses instead of trying to determine symbolic host names.

description: Perform highly detailed DNS (Domain Name System) lookups and query DNS name servers.
Displays the state of the working directory and the staging area in a Git repository.
Transfers data from or to a server using supported protocols, commonly used to test REST APIs.

flags.meaning: Include the HTTP response headers in the output.

description: Recursively searches through all files in the current directory for a specific text pattern.

flags.meaning: Search recursively down into all subdirectories.
Prefix each line of output with the 1-based line number within its input file.
Select only those lines containing matches that form whole words.

description: Extracts the contents of a compressed gzip tarball archive into the current directory.

flags.meaning: Extract files from an archive.
Filter the archive through gzip (decompress).
Use archive file or device ARCHIVE.

description: Prints all or part of the environment variables currently loaded into the shell session.
Clears the terminal screen, pushing previous output out of view to give you a clean workspace.
Displays the command history list with line numbers, allowing you to see previously executed commands.
Downloads files from the web directly to the current directory using HTTP, HTTPS, or FTP.

flags.meaning: Specifies the exact output document name for the downloaded file.

description: Formats and displays the system's official manual pages for a specific command.
Lists the contents of directories in a tree-like format, making complex structures easy to read.

flags.meaning: Limits the maximum display depth of the directory tree to 2 levels down.

description: Displays the exact process (and PID) that is currently listening on a specific network port (e.g., port 80).

flags.meaning: Display only listening sockets.
Show the process using the socket.
Display TCP sockets only.
Show numerical addresses instead of trying to resolve service names.

description: Displays or sets the current system date and time in the terminal.
Creates a temporary custom shortcut for a longer command. (Note: To make it permanent, add this line to your ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc file).
Displays a visual, formatted calendar grid of the current month in the terminal.

flags.meaning: Display the previous, current, and next month side-by-side.

description: Counts and displays the total number of lines in a file.

flags.meaning: Print only the newline counts (total lines), ignoring words and bytes.

description: Executes a program periodically, showing output in fullscreen to watch it change over time.

flags.meaning: Specify the update interval in seconds (default is 2).

description: Generates a highly secure, cryptographically strong random password encoded in Base64.

flags.meaning: Perform base64 encoding on the output so it can be copied as text.
The number of random bytes to generate.

description: Clears the local DNS resolver cache on systemd-based Linux. (Note: On macOS, use 'sudo dscacheutil -flushcache').

flags.meaning: Instructs resolvectl to clear all cached DNS records.

description: Encodes standard text or files into a Base64 string, commonly used in web tokens and data transfers.
Displays the default routing gateway and the primary network interface currently being used for outbound traffic. (Note: On macOS, use 'route -n get default').

flags.meaning: Filters the routing table to only display the default 0.0.0.0 route.

description: Displays the current state and connection details of all network interfaces managed by NetworkManager.
Downloads and installs a package using the Homebrew package manager.
Searches Homebrew for a specific software package or cask.
